title: Using digital droplet PCR to detect rare Wolbachia endosymbionts
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"21u9k","text":"This protocol details three sensitive digital droplet polymerase chain reaction (ddPCR) assays for quantifying Wolbachia endosymbionts. These assays reliably detect as few as 12 Wolbachia gene copies per 20 µL reaction, eliminating the need for sample pooling and preserving valuable individual-level resolution. Each assay targets the single-copy Wolbachia gene ftsZ, using primers and a FAM-labeled probe designed to recognize sequences conserved across 106 supergroup A Wolbachia strains. The singleplex ftsZ-ddPCR assay measures absolute Wolbachia gene copy number per sample, the duplex ftsZ/spike-ddPCR assay quantifies Wolbachia gene copies while correcting for DNA purification efficiency using a proprietary DNA spike-in control, and the duplex ftsZ/mid1-ddPCR assay determines Wolbachia density per host cell by normalizing ftsZ copies to a conserved host gene (mid1). The mid1 assay is designed with homology to 53 Drosophila, Scaptomyza, and Zaprionus species, where the mid1 non-coding region is highly conserved. These sensitive and versatile ddPCR assays will empower researchers to investigate Wolbachia dynamics at unprecedented resolution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":111,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":178,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":348,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":363,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":473,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":507,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":542,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":592,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":626,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":754,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":759,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":787,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":834,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":872,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":926,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":938,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":954,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":983,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":1110,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Remove the following reagents from the freezer and allow to thaw On ice for 00:30:00 : ddPCR Supermix for Probes (no dUTP) Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863023 Water, molecular biology grade MIDSCI Catalog #KC248796 Primers and probes, as appropriate DNA samples

Select one of the following assays. Create a master mix in a Centrifuge tubes, 1.5 mL, safe-lock, PCR clean Eppendorf Catalog #22363212 based on the described composition. The final volume of each assay will be 20 µL , including 2 µL of DNA template. Calculate a 10% excess.

ftsZ / mid1 -ddPCR assay reaction composition: 10 µL ddPCR Supermix for Probes (no dUTP) Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863023 7 µL Water, molecular biology grade MIDSCI Catalog #KC248796 0.5 µL ftsZ oligo mixture with FAM-labeled probe 0.5 µL mid1 oligo mixture with HEX-labeled probe 2 µL DNA template The ftsZ / mid1 -ddPCR assay is used to measure Wolbachia density (relative abundance). This assay is useful when: The number of Wolbachia cells per host cell is more relevant than the total Wolbachia count. Host cell numbers vary between treatment groups or samples.

ftsZ /spike-ddPCR assay reaction composition: 10 µL ddPCR Supermix for Probes (no dUTP) Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863023 6 µL Water, molecular biology grade MIDSCI Catalog #KC248796 0.5 µL ftsZ oligo mixture with FAM-labeled probe 1 µL DNA spike-in control primer mixture 0.5 µL DNA spike-in control HEX-labeled probe 2 µL DNA template The ftsZ /spike-ddPCR assay is used to measure Wolbachia titer (absolute abundance). This assay is useful when: Technical variations, such as DNA extraction efficiency, can significantly impact results. The biological effect size is small and can be masked by technical noise. There is a need to back-calculate Wolbachia abundance in the original sample.
Vortex the master mix for 00:00:30 to mix.
Dispense 18 µL of the master mix into each well in a PCR plate, 96 well Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863005 .
Add 2.0 µL DNA template to each reaction. Add 2.0 µL Water, molecular biology grade MIDSCI Catalog #KC248796 to no template control reactions (NTCs)
Use the Microseal “B” adhesive seal to seal the plate. Use a roller to ensure the seal is tight. Mix thoroughly by vortexing the plate for 00:00:10 .

Insert a DG8 cartridge Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1864007 into a DG8 cartridge holder Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863051 . Check that the orientation of the cartridge is aligned properly in the cartridge holder. Slide the edges of the holder toward the center to snap close.

Peel away the adhesive seal to expose the sample. Be gentle while removing the seal to avoid aerosolizing the samples. By pipetting, transfer 19.5 µL of each reaction mix to the sample wells of a DG8 cartridge. Avoid pipetting air bubbles into the DG8 cartridge. Check each sample in the DG8 cartridge for air bubbles. Remove air bubbles by pipetting slowly using a different tip. Air bubbles can interfere with droplet generation.
By pipetting, add 70 µL Droplet generation oil for probes Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863005 to the oil wells in the DG8 cartridge Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1864007 .
Insert the DG8 cartridge holder Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863051 into the droplet generator. Close the lid. Wait approximately 00:02:00 for droplets to be generated
While waiting, go to step #10 to prepare the next set of eight reactions.

Open the droplet generator. Replace the cartridge holder containing droplets with the cartridge holder that was prepared while waiting. Close the lid to start droplet generation. Remove the cartridge from the first cartridge holder and check for droplets. By pipetting, transfer the droplet well ( 40 µL ) from the DG8 cartridge Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1864007 to a clean PCR plate, 96 well, ddPCR Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #12001925 . If droplet generating is successful, the wells appear opaque. Pipette slowly to avoid shearing the droplets.
Transfer the sealed PCR plate containing droplets to a thermal cycler.

Run the following program with a ramp rate of 1 °C per 00:00:01 and with a heated lid of 105 °C . 1 cycle of: 95 °C for 00:10:00 to activate polymerase and denature DNA. 50 cycles of: 94 °C for 00:01:00 to denature DNA. 60 °C for 00:02:00 to anneal primers and replicate amplicons. 1 cycle of: 98 °C for 00:10:00 to inactivate polymerase. 12 °C forever to store amplicons until use.
Vortex reagents for 00:00:30 to mix.

ftsZ -ddPCR assay reaction composition: 10 µL ddPCR Supermix for Probes (no dUTP) Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863023 7.5 µL Water, molecular biology grade MIDSCI Catalog #KC248796 0.5 µL ftsZ oligo mixture with FAM-labeled probe 2 µL DNA template The ftsZ -ddPCR assay is used to measure Wolbachia titers (absolute abundance). This assay is useful when: Absolute target abundance in the original sample is not a primary concern. Technical variations, such as DNA extraction efficiency, are evenly distributed across treatment groups. Biological variation between treatment groups is substantial enough to overcome the impact of technical variations.

Centrifuge at 2204 rcf, Room temperature, 2 minutes to bring contents to the bottom of each well. After centrifugation, check that there are no bubbles across the wells. If there are bubbles, repeat the centrifugation.
Centrifuge at 2000 rcf, Room temperature, 10 seconds to bring contents to the bottom of each tube.

Transfer the assembled plate to the droplet reader, and secure it with the clamps in the machine. Check that well A1 is in the top-left position and close the lid. If the plate is not secured properly, the sampling needle can stick to the lid and damage the instrument.
Open the QX Manager software on the attached computer.

If the droplet reader has not been used in the past week, it is important that the system be primed using the QX Manager software. Select the "System Utilities" tab. Select "Prime." Select "Yes" to begin. Select "OK" after priming is complete.
Add a plate in the QX Manager software.

Open the Plate Configuration window. Select the "Add Plate" tab. Select the plus icon beside "Add Plate." Select "Configure Plate" to open the plate configuration window with three tabs: "Plate Information," "Well Selection," and "Well Information."

Record general plate information. Select the "Plate Information" tab. Add a name for the plate and record it in your lab notebook. Define the supermix used in the reactions Define the file name and save the plate.
Identify the wells in the plate that contain droplets. Select the "Well Selection" tab. Select the wells that contain droplets. Click "Include Selected Wells."

Define the type of experiment and the contents of the wells. Select the "Well Information" tab. Click "Experiment Type" and select the appropriate type of experiment. Enter sample descriptions into the "Sample Description 1" through "Sample Description 4" fields. Select the appropriate "Sample Type" for each well. Click "Supermix" and select "Supermix for Probes." Click "Assay Type" and select "Single target per channel." Enter the target (e.g., ftsZ , mid1 , spike) in the "Target Name" field. Select the appropriate "Target Type" for each target. Click "Signal Ch1" and select "FAM." Click "Signal Ch2" and select "HEX" if reactions include mid1 or spike and "none" otherwise.

Select "Save" and "Start Run" to begin reading droplets The software performs a prerequisites check to ensure that the instrument is ready. This will tell you if ddPCR Droplet Reader Oil Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863004 needs to be added or if waste needs to be emptied. Always add 50 mL of 10 % (v/v) bleach to the waste container after emptying.

After the run is complete, save files associated with the run. Click "Save" to save the run. Select the "Data Analysis" tab. Click "Error Model" a select "Poisson" and "95%." Select the "Data Table" tab, ensure all columns are reported, and save it as a csv file. Select the "Run Information" tab and save the table. Select the "Reports" tab, click "Select All," and save the report as a PDF. Select the "1D Amplitude," "2D Amplitude," and "Concentration" tabs to save the plots associated with the run.

Once all the samples have been transferred, seal the PCR plate with a PCR plate heat seal, foil, pierceable Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1814040 using a thermal PCR plate sealer set to heat the foil at 180 °C for 00:00:05 . When using a PX1 plate sealer, ensure the PCR plate is correctly aligned with the heat sealer. Always position the foil with the red line facing upward to prevent it from sticking to the machine and causing damage.
